goal: Give Bluepilot a separate TypeScript subpackage for the upcoming Builder migration without moving any soulmatch Builder module yet.
scope_out: Do not move, copy, or import any soulmatch Builder module.
Do not change Bluepilot root package.json or tools/*.cjs.
Do not add database, provider, network, GitHub, or maya-core runtime calls.
Do not start Tier-1 module migration in this task.
claims: builder/ is a self-contained TypeScript ESM subpackage.
builder/tsconfig.json supports NodeNext ESM and .js import specifiers used by the soulmatch Builder modules.
builder/tests/smoke.test.ts proves TypeScript execution through tsx and node:test.
The root CJS governance/WLP world remains untouched.
docs/BUILDER_MIGRATION_MATRIX.md records the corrected migration tiers and notes that the Tier-1 import closure must be machine-checked before module movement.
assumptions: The Builder should live under a separate Bluepilot subpackage instead of turning the Bluepilot root into a TypeScript app.
Keeping a builder/package-lock.json is acceptable for reproducibility.
The first real migration block will be cut only after the TS foundation is verified.
dod: builder/package.json declares type=module and scripts for test and typecheck.
builder/tsconfig.json uses NodeNext module and moduleResolution.
builder/src/smoke.ts exports a trivial function and builder/src/index.ts re-exports it via a .js specifier.
npm install and npm test inside builder/ succeed.
npm run typecheck inside builder/ succeeds.
Root package.json and tools/*.cjs remain unchanged.
No soulmatch Builder module is copied into Bluepilot.
stop_conditions: A root package.json change becomes necessary.
A soulmatch Builder module move or copy becomes necessary.
A database, provider, network, GitHub, or maya-core runtime dependency becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BP-125 completed Bluepilot's anchors. The builder migration must start with a separate TypeScript subpackage because Bluepilot root is CJS/governance-only while the soulmatch Builder is TypeScript ESM with .js import specifiers.
